system: You are an expert puzzle-solver specializing in the NYT Connections game. Follow the rules strictly and provide structured responses.
user:
HOW TO PLAY 1. You are given 16 words that form 4 hidden groups of 4 related words each. 2. Sort all 16 words into the 4 groups they belong to. 3. This is ONE-SHOT: you submit ALL 4 groups in a SINGLE response. There is no feedback and no retry. 4. Every word must be used exactly once. Each group must contain EXACTLY 4 words.
TRAPS The puzzle may contain TRAP SETS: 4 (or more) clues that form a convincing category but do NOT form one of the real groups. In the block, pick the single most likely trap set and list EXACTLY 4 of its words — or state N/A; not all puzzles have traps.
SCORING Base: 1 point per correctly matched group; matching all 4 earns 3 points. Because getting exactly 3 groups right forces the 4th, base scores are 0, 1, 2, or 3. Trap bonus (+2): - +2 if your trap set is a genuine designed trap (when a trap spans 5+ clues, any 4 of them count). - +2 if you state N/A and the puzzle truly has no traps. - An incorrect trap claim earns nothing — do not guess wildly. Only your FIRST listed trap set is judged. Omitting the traps block scores no bonus. Maximum: 5 points per puzzle. If your answer is not in a valid format — not exactly 4 groups of 4 words, or the 16 words do not exactly match the puzzle's words — you score 0, including the trap bonus.
RESPONSE FORMAT: Provide your response in this exact structure:
[Your reasoning]
[Four lines, each with EXACTLY four words, ALL CAPS, comma-separated — one line per group]
[ONE line with EXACTLY four words, ALL CAPS, comma-separated — your single most likely trap set. Or the single line N/A if you believe there are no traps.]
[A number between 0.0 and 1.0 indicating your confidence in this answer]
